commit.message:
fix(api): use client secret as fallback for webhook signature verification

WHOOP (and potentially other providers) use the OAuth client secret as
the webhook signing key — there is no separate webhook secret. The
inbound route now falls back to <PROVIDER>_CLIENT_SECRET when
<PROVIDER>_WEBHOOK_SECRET is not set.

This means WHOOP webhooks will work out of the box without needing to
set WHOOP_WEBHOOK_SECRET separately — the existing WHOOP_CLIENT_SECRET
is used for HMAC-SHA256 signature verification.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add migration for inbound_webhook_logs table

The table schema was added to Drizzle but no migration file was generated,
so the table doesn't exist in deployed databases. This migration creates
the table with all indexes and the FK constraint.

feat(external-review): external review backend (ralph-ext.sh) (#74)

* feat: add ralph-ext.sh entry point with GitHub Projects V2 integration (#64)

Implements the scaffolding entry point for external review workflows:

- Parses CLI args: ./ralph-ext.sh <issue> --label=<slug>
- Loads config from ralph.toml using the same toml_get() pattern as ralph.sh
- Validates gh token has project scope early with a clear error message
- Finds a GitHub Projects V2 board by title matching the --label slug
- Reads the first "Todo" item (sorted by title for task number prefixes)
- Updates item status to "In Progress" / "Done" via GraphQL mutations
- Creates feat/<label> branch from origin/main if it doesn't exist
- Sets up and tears down a git worktree (same pattern as ralph.sh)
- Exits cleanly if no Todo items found; errors if no board matches

Extracted reusable shell functions: project_find_board(),
project_next_todo(), project_set_status().

- Run Copilot CLI to write code, commit, push, and open a PR

* feat: request Copilot review, poll for result, and merge on approval (#66)

After PR creation, request a Copilot review via the GitHub API, poll
every 60 seconds for up to 20 minutes, and handle the result:

- "no new comments" → merge PR, set project status to Done, sync worktree
- N comments → stop with message (fix loop not yet implemented)
- Poll timeout → re-request once, then fail with clear error

Adds request_copilot_review() and poll_copilot_review() shell functions.
Rate-limit friendly: ≤20 API calls per review cycle.

commit.message:
fix: detect and adopt orphaned gateway on port conflict in cmd_serve

When the gateway PID file was stale/missing but a gateway process was
still listening on the port (e.g. after a crash or reboot), cmd_serve
would:
  1. Start a new Python gateway that immediately crashed (port in use)
  2. Poll the health check — which passed because the OLD gateway answered
  3. Show '✅ running' without a valid PID file
  4. Every subsequent ps/run/shutdown would see 'not running'

Fix: before starting a new gateway, check if the port is already serving.
  - If yes: use lsof to find the owning PID, write it to gateway.pid, and
    return 0 (adopt the orphan instead of trying to replace it).
  - If no: start fresh as before.
  - After health check, verify the PID file was actually written; if not,
    surface a clear error instead of silently succeeding with a stale state.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: write PID file after port bind; backend PID files for clean shutdown

Bug 1 - PID file race in gateway:
  serve() wrote the PID file BEFORE binding the port. If the port was
  already occupied (orphaned old gateway), the new process wrote a dead
  PID and crashed immediately. The health check succeeded because the old
  gateway answered, leaving a stale PID file pointing to a dead process.
  Every subsequent 'ps' or 'run' removed the stale file and said 'not running'.
  Fix: write PID file AFTER ThreadedServer() binds successfully.

Bug 2 - Orphaned backends survive gateway restart:
  When the gateway died without SIGTERM (OS kill, crash), its mlx_lm.server
  children became orphans. A new gateway could not register them and would
  try to start new instances on already-occupied ports.
  Fix: each ModelBackend writes its PID to ~/.mlx/backends/<port>.pid at
  start and removes it on clean stop. cmd_shutdown reads those files and
  kills each PID explicitly, even if the gateway PID file is gone.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs(agents): extract PR workflow skills from AGENTS.MD (#45)

* chore(skills): add create-pr and fix-review-comments Copilot skills

Extract two on-demand workflows from AGENTS.MD into dedicated VS Code
Copilot skills under .github/skills/:

- fix-review-comments: full PR review procedure (fetch comments, address
  each, reply in batch), reply quality guidelines, and rejection flow.
  Previously embedded verbatim in AGENTS.MD.

- create-pr: branch naming, logical commit grouping, conventional commit
  format reference (types table, scopes, rules), PR description template,
  and push/gh pr create invocation. New — workflow not previously
  documented.

Both skills auto-load via keyword matching in Copilot and appear as slash
commands (/fix-review-comments, /create-pr). The full procedure is also
reachable by Claude Code CLI via explicit file paths referenced in AGENTS.MD.

* docs(agents): trim AGENTS.MD and add skill references

Reduce always-on context loaded on every request by ~50 lines while
preserving full cross-tool compatibility:

- Remove verbose agent-workspace examples (code blocks, PR script file
  list) — keep the essential rules as concise bullets (~13 lines vs ~50)
- Replace the 20-line PR Review Workflow with a 5-line condensed version
  referencing the new fix-review-comments skill
- Add a 5-line Creating PRs section referencing the new create-pr skill
- Remove PR-specific commands from Useful Commands (now in the skill)

AGENTS.MD drops from ~250 to ~195 lines. Claude Code CLI retains the
core procedure steps inline; Copilot users get full skill auto-loading
and slash commands.

* Update .github/skills/fix-review-comments/SKILL.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(skills): warn against shell for writing commit msg and PR body

Steps 3 and 4 of create-pr now explicitly require using the file
creation/edit tool instead of shell (echo, heredoc, cat >) to write
agent-workspace/commit-msg.txt and pr-body.md. Terminal encoding
corrupts multi-line content when written via shell, as observed when
the PR description for #45 was mangled.

The same rule exists in AGENTS.MD but placing it at the point of
action in the workflow makes it harder to miss.

fix: remove --chat-template-args from backend; use /no_think in system message

--chat-template-args '{"enable_thinking": false}' passed to mlx_lm.server
was corrupting Qwen3's chat template processing, causing the model to
respond with a hardcoded JSON error to every single prompt:
  {"error": "Invalid request. Please provide a valid input or clarify your question."}

The flag is removed entirely from _build_command(). Thinking is now
disabled per-conversation via the /no_think soft-switch in the system
message (chat_repl) or prepended to the user message (cmd_chat --no-think).
Both are supported natively by Qwen3 and are no-ops for other models.

NOTE: running backends started with the old flag must be restarted.
Stop them with: mlx-server stop <model>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add default system prompt in chat REPL to prevent JSON output mode

Qwen3 with mlx_lm.server's enable_thinking:false chat template arg can
push the model into a structured/JSON response mode when no system
message is provided. The model then responds with JSON to all prompts
requests.

Adding an explicit system message anchors the model in natural language
mode and overrides whatever default mlx_lm injects from the template.

Note: the original 'list index out of range' crash (fixed earlier) was
masking this - the JSON output existed from the very first session.

fix: redirect chat REPL stdin from /dev/tty to prevent bash script corruption

When bash executes a large script file, it reads it in chunks. If a
subprocess (Python chat REPL) reads from stdin (fd 0) while bash is
mid-script, Python can consume bytes that are part of the next chunk
of the script file, leaving bash with a garbled read position. This
manifests as: 'syntax error near unexpected token ;;' after /bye exits.

Fix: pass < /dev/tty so Python reads directly from the terminal,
leaving bash's internal script file descriptor untouched.
